reasons: High sustained return on invested capital
High sustained return on invested capital

stability.note: True management stability needs tenure/history data; Yahoo Finance does not always provide it reliably.

management.ownership_note: Ownership is directly from Yahoo Finance when available.

sustained_roic.note: NOPAT / Invested Capital. Uses tax rate from statements when available.

market_share.note: Needs company revenue and TAM/market size from an external source.

brand_value.note: Brand value requires an external brand valuation source.

cost_advantage.note: Needs peer or industry gross margin to assess cost advantage accurately.

product_differentiation.note: True differentiation needs product, brand, and peer comparison data. Higher gross margin can indicate differentiation or pricing power. Premium valuation can sometimes reflect perceived differentiation.

switching_costs.note: Switching costs are best measured using retention, recurring revenue, or contract data.

network_effects.note: Not enough data to infer network effects.

regulatory_barriers.note: Sector='Financial Services', Industry='Credit Services'. Real regulatory barriers require legal or industry research.

management_ownership.note: Direct insider ownership from Yahoo Finance.

capital_allocation_efficiency.note: Uses free cash flow conversion, capex intensity, and a rough invested-capital return proxy.

management_stability.note: True management stability needs tenure/history data; Yahoo Finance does not always provide it reliably.

tam.note: TAM is not available from Yahoo Finance alone.

market_concentration.note: Needs market structure data, competitor revenues, or a direct concentration index.

customer_concentration.note: Customer concentration typically comes from annual reports or segment disclosures.

threat_of_new_entrants.interpretation: Lower is better for the company.

supplier_power.interpretation: Lower is better for the company.

buyer_power.interpretation: Lower is better for the company.

threat_of_substitutes.interpretation: Lower is better for the company.

industry_rivalry.interpretation: Lower is better for the company.

capital_allocation_efficiency.note: Uses free cash flow conversion, capex intensity, and a rough invested-capital return proxy.

ownership.comment: Higher insider holding generally suggests stronger incentive alignment.
